title: polemonium boreale var. macranthum
intro: Polemonium boreale var. macranthum, also known as the northern Jacob's ladder or western sky pilot, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its roots, which contain a compound called cyclopamine, a potent inhibitor of the hedgehog signaling pathway, which has been shown to have potential anti-cancer properties. The health benefits of Polemonium boreale var. macranthum include its potential to prevent or treat certain types of cancer, such as basal cell carcinoma, and its possible anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots and can be used in various forms, including tinctures, teas, and capsules, although its use should be closely monitored under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
